records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1455 - Sama’a bin Mehran narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"There is no prayer on the two Eids (Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha) except with an Imam, but if you pray alone, there is no issue."
records.arabicText: 1455 - وَ رَوَى سَمَاعَةُ بْنُ مِهْرَانَ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «لاَ صَلاَةَ فِي اَلْعِيدَيْنِ إِلاَّ مَعَ إِمَامٍ وَ إِنْ صَلَّيْتَ وَحْدَكَ فَلاَ بَأْسَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1455 - Sama’a bin Mehran narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no prayer on the two Eids (Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha) except with an Imam, but if you pray alone, there is no issue."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1456 - Zurarah bin A'yan narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
"There is no prayer on the day of Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha except with a just Imam”.
records.arabicText: 1456 - وَ رَوَى زُرَارَةُ بْنُ أَعْيَنَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «لاَ صَلاَةَ يَوْمَ اَلْفِطْرِ وَ اَلْأَضْحَى إِلاَّ مَعَ إِمَامٍ عَادِلٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1456 - Zurarah bin A'yan narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no prayer on the day of Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha except with a just Imam”.

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1457 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the prayers of Eid al-Adha and Eid al-Fitr, and He said:
"Pray them as two units (rak'ahs), either in congregation or individually, and say seven takbirs (Allahu Akbar) in the first rak'ah and five in the second”.
records.arabicText: 1457 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنْ صَلاَةِ اَلْأَضْحَى وَ اَلْفِطْرِ فَقَالَ «صَلِّهِمَا رَكْعَتَيْنِ فِي جَمَاعَةٍ أَوْ فِي غَيْرِ جَمَاعَةٍ وَ كَبِّرْ سَبْعاً وَ خَمْساً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1457 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the prayers of Eid al-Adha and Eid al-Fitr, and He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Pray them as two units (rak'ahs), either in congregation or individually, and say seven takbirs (Allahu Akbar) in the first rak'ah and five in the second”.

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1458 - Mansur ibn Hazim narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"My father (as) fell ill on the day of Eid al-Adha, so he prayed two units (rak'ahs) in his home and then offered the sacrifice."
records.arabicText: 1458 - وَ رَوَى مَنْصُورُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «مَرِضَ أَبِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَوْمَ اَلْأَضْحَى فَصَلَّى فِي بَيْتِهِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ ثُمَّ ضَحَّى.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1458 - Mansur ibn Hazim narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "My father (as) fell ill on the day of Eid al-Adha, so he prayed two units (rak'ahs) in his home and then offered the sacrifice."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1459 - Ja'far ibn Bashir narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"Whoever does not attend the congregation of people for the Eid prayers should perform the ritual bath (ghusl), apply whatever perfume is available, and then pray at home alone as he would pray in congregation”.
records.arabicText: 1459 - وَ رَوَى جَعْفَرُ بْنُ بَشِيرٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «مَنْ لَمْ يَشْهَدْ جَمَاعَةَ اَلنَّاسِ فِي اَلْعِيدَيْنِ فَلْيَغْتَسِلْ وَ لْيَتَطَيَّبْ بِمَا وَجَدَ وَ يُصَلِّي فِي بَيْتِهِ وَحْدَهُ كَمَا يُصَلِّي فِي جَمَاعَةٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1459 - Ja'far ibn Bashir narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever does not attend the congregation of people for the Eid prayers should perform the ritual bath (ghusl), apply whatever perfume is available, and then pray at home alone as he would pray in congregation”.

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1460 - Harun ibn Hamzah al-Ghanawi narrates from Imam Jafar Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Going out on the day of Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha to the open area (for prayer) is good for whoever is able to go."
Harun asked: "What if a person is sick and unable to go out, should he pray at home?"
Imam (as) replied: "No."
records.arabicText: 1460 - وَ رَوَى هَارُونُ بْنُ حَمْزَةَ اَلْغَنَوِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «اَلْخُرُوجُ يَوْمَ اَلْفِطْرِ وَ اَلْأَضْحَى إِلَى اَلْجَبَّانَةِ حَسَنٌ لِمَنِ اِسْتَطَاعَ اَلْخُرُوجَ إِلَيْهَا» قَالَ فَقُلْتُ أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ كَانَ مَرِيضاً لاَ يَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ يَخْرُجَ أَ يُصَلِّي فِي بَيْتِهِ فَقَالَ «لاَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1460 - Harun ibn Hamzah al-Ghanawi narrates from Imam Jafar Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Going out on the day of Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha to the open area (for prayer) is good for whoever is able to go."
Harun asked: "What if a person is sick and unable to go out, should he pray at home?"
Imam (as) replied: "No."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1461 - Ibn al-Mughira narrated from al-Qasim ibn al-Walid, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about the Ghusl (ritual purification bath) of Eid al-Adha.
Imam (as) said: "It is obligatory except for those in Mina."
records.arabicText: 1461 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ اَلْمُغِيرَةِ عَنِ اَلْقَاسِمِ بْنِ اَلْوَلِيدِ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ غُسْلِ اَلْأَضْحَى، قَالَ «وَاجِبٌ إِلاَّ بِمِنًى.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1461 - Ibn al-Mughira narrated from al-Qasim ibn al-Walid, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about the Ghusl (ritual purification bath) of Eid al-Adha.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is obligatory except for those in Mina."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1462 - It has been narrated:
"The Ghusl (ritual purification bath) for the two Eids is a Sunnah (recommended practice)."
records.arabicText: 1462 - وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّ غُسْلَ اَلْعِيدَيْنِ سُنَّةٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1462 - It has been narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Ghusl (ritual purification bath) for the two Eids is a Sunnah (recommended practice)."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1463 - Al-Halabi narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
"I asked him about whether a woman is required to perform Ghusl (ritual purification bath) on Jumu’ah, on the day of Eid al-Fitr, on the day of Eid al-Adha, and on the day of Arafah.
Imam (as) said: 'Yes, She is required to perform all of these Ghusls.'"
records.arabicText: 1463 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ عَلَيْهَا غُسْلُ يَوْمِ اَلْجُمُعَةِ وَ اَلْفِطْرِ وَ اَلْأَضْحَى وَ يَوْمِ عَرَفَةَ، قَالَ «نَعَمْ عَلَيْهَا اَلْغُسْلُ كُلُّهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1463 - Al-Halabi narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked him about whether a woman is required to perform Ghusl (ritual purification bath) on Jumu’ah, on the day of Eid al-Fitr, on the day of Eid al-Adha, and on the day of Arafah.
Imam (as) said: 'Yes, She is required to perform all of these Ghusls.'"

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.1464 - Imam Ali (as) would eat on the day of Eid al-Fitr before heading to the prayer area, but on the day of Eid al-Adha, he would not eat until after performing the ritual sacrifice.
records.arabicText: 1464 - وَ كَانَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَأْكُلُ يَوْمَ اَلْفِطْرِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَغْدُوَ إِلَى اَلْمُصَلَّى وَ لاَ يَأْكُلُ يَوْمَ اَلْأَضْحَى حَتَّى يَذْبَحَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1464 - Imam Ali (as) would eat on the day of Eid al-Fitr before heading to the prayer area, but on the day of Eid al-Adha,
records.thaqalaynMatn: he would not eat until after performing the ritual sacrifice.

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1465 - Hariz narrated from Zurarah that Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said: "Do not leave your house on the day of Eid al-Fitr until you have eaten something, and do not eat anything on the day of Eid al-Adha except from your own sacrifice, if you are able. If you are not able, then you are excused."
Abu Ja'far (as) also said: "Amir al-Mu’minin (as) would not eat on the day of Eid al-Adha until he had eaten from his own sacrifice, and he would not leave on the day of Eid al-Fitr until he had eaten and paid the Zakat al-Fitr."
He added, "This is also our practice."
records.arabicText: 1465 - وَ رَوَى حَرِيزٌ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «لاَ تَخْرُجْ يَوْمَ اَلْفِطْرِ حَتَّى تَطْعَمَ شَيْئاً وَ لاَ تَأْكُلْ يَوْمَ اَلْأَضْحَى شَيْئاً إِلاَّ مِنْ هَدْيِكَ وَ أُضْحِيَّتِكَ إِنْ قَوِيتَ عَلَيْهِ وَ إِنْ لَمْ تَقْوَ فَمَعْذُورٌ» قَالَ وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «كَانَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ لاَ يَأْكُلُ يَوْمَ اَلْأَضْحَى شَيْئاً حَتَّى يَأْكُلَ مِنْ أُضْحِيَّتِهِ وَ لاَ يَخْرُجُ يَوْمَ اَلْفِطْرِ حَتَّى يَطْعَمَ وَ يُؤَدِّيَ اَلْفِطْرَةَ ثُمَّ قَالَ وَ كَذَلِكَ نَحْنُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1465 - Hariz narrated from Zurarah that Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Do not leave your house on the day of Eid al-Fitr until you have eaten something, and do not eat anything on the day of Eid al-Adha except from your own sacrifice, if you are able. If you are not able, then you are excused."
Abu Ja'far (as) also said: "Amir al-Mu’minin (as) would not eat on the day of Eid al-Adha until he had eaten from his own sacrifice, and he would not leave on the day of Eid al-Fitr until he had eaten and paid the Zakat al-Fitr."
He added, "This is also our practice."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1466 - Hafs ibn Ghiyath narrated from Ja'far ibn Muhammad from his father (as) that He said:
"The Sunnah (tradition) for the people of cities is to go out from their cities for the two Eid prayers, except for the people of Mecca, who pray in the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram)."
records.arabicText: 1466 - وَ رَوَى حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «اَلسُّنَّةُ عَلَى أَهْلِ اَلْأَمْصَارِ أَنْ يَبْرُزُوا مِنْ أَمْصَارِهِمْ فِي اَلْعِيدَيْنِ إِلاَّ أَهْلَ مَكَّةَ فَإِنَّهُمْ يُصَلُّونَ فِي اَلْمَسْجِدِ اَلْحَرَامِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1466 - Hafs ibn Ghiyath narrated from Ja'far ibn Muhammad from his father (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Sunnah (tradition) for the people of cities is to go out from their cities for the two Eid prayers, except for the people of Mecca, who pray in the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram)."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1467 - Ali ibn Ri'ab narrated from Abu Basir from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"It is not appropriate for the Eid prayers to be performed in a covered mosque or in a house; rather, they should be prayed in the open air or in an exposed place."
records.arabicText: 1467 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ رِئَابٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «لاَ يَنْبَغِي أَنْ تُصَلَّى صَلاَةُ اَلْعِيدَيْنِ فِي مَسْجِدٍ مُسَقَّفٍ وَ لاَ فِي بَيْتٍ إِنَّمَا تُصَلَّى فِي اَلصَّحْرَاءِ أَوْ فِي مَكَانٍ بَارِزٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1467 - Ali ibn Ri'ab narrated from Abu Basir from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is not appropriate for the Eid prayers to be performed in a covered mosque or in a house; rather, they should be prayed in the open air or in an exposed place."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1468 - Al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father (as) that when he would go out on the days of Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha, he would refuse to have a carpet (or mat) brought to him to pray on.
He would say, "This is a day when the Messenger of Allah (sw) would go out until he was exposed to the horizons of the sky, then he would place his forehead on the earth."
records.arabicText: 1468 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ إِذَا خَرَجَ يَوْمَ اَلْفِطْرِ وَ اَلْأَضْحَى أَبَى أَنْ يُؤْتَى بِطِنْفِسَةٍ يُصَلِّي عَلَيْهَا يَقُولُ «هَذَا يَوْمٌ كَانَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ يَخْرُجُ فِيهِ حَتَّى يَبْرُزَ لآِفَاقِ اَلسَّمَاءِ ثُمَّ يَضَعُ جَبْهَتَهُ عَلَى اَلْأَرْضِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1468 - Al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father (as) that when he would go out on the days of Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha, he would refuse to have a carpet (or mat) brought to him to pray on.
records.thaqalaynMatn: He would say, "This is a day when the Messenger of Allah (sw) would go out until he was exposed to the horizons of the sky, then he would place his forehead on the earth."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1469 - Isma'il ibn Jabir narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
"I asked him about the prayer of the two Eids (Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha), whether there is an Adhan (call to prayer) or Iqamah (second call to prayer) for them."
Imam (as) said: "There is neither Adhan nor Iqamah for them, but it is announced 'As-Salah (the prayer), As-Salah (the prayer)' three times. There is no pulpit (minbar) for them; the minbar is not moved from its place.
However, a structure similar to a pulpit is made from clay for the Imam to stand upon and deliver a sermon, then he comes down."
records.arabicText: 1469 - وَ رَوَى إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لَهُ أَ رَأَيْتَ صَلاَةَ اَلْعِيدَيْنِ هَلْ فِيهِمَا أَذَانٌ وَ إِقَامَةٌ قَالَ «لَيْسَ فِيهِمَا أَذَانٌ وَ لاَ إِقَامَةٌ وَ لَكِنْ يُنَادَى اَلصَّلاَةَ اَلصَّلاَةَ ثَلاَثَ مَرَّاتٍ وَ لَيْسَ فِيهِمَا مِنْبَرٌ اَلْمِنْبَرُ لاَ يُحَرَّكُ مِنْ مَوْضِعِهِ وَ لَكِنْ يُصْنَعُ لِلْإِمَامِ شِبْهُ اَلْمِنْبَرِ مِنْ طِينٍ فَيَقُومُ عَلَيْهِ فَيَخْطُبُ اَلنَّاسَ ثُمَّ يَنْزِلُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1469 - Isma'il ibn Jabir narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked him about the prayer of the two Eids (Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha), whether there is an Adhan (call to prayer) or Iqamah (second call to prayer) for them."
Imam (as) said: "There is neither Adhan nor Iqamah for them, but it is announced 'As-Salah (the prayer), As-Salah (the prayer)' three times. There is no pulpit (minbar) for them; the minbar is not moved from its place.
However, a structure similar to a pulpit is made from clay for the Imam to stand upon and deliver a sermon, then he comes down."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1470 - Hariz narrated from Zurarah from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"Do not make up the Witr prayer of your night, meaning on the two Eids, if you missed it until you have prayed the midday (Dhuhr) prayer on that day."
records.arabicText: 1470 - وَ رَوَى حَرِيزٌ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «لاَ تَقْضِ وَتْرَ لَيْلَتِكَ يَعْنِي فِي اَلْعِيدَيْنِ إِنْ كَانَ فَاتَكَ حَتَّى تُصَلِّيَ اَلزَّوَالَ فِي ذَلِكَ اَلْيَوْمِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1470 - Hariz narrated from Zurarah from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not make up the Witr prayer of your night, meaning on the two Eids, if you missed it until you have prayed the midday (Dhuhr) prayer on that day."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1471 - Muhammad ibn al-Fadl al-Hashimi narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"There are two (units of prayer) from the Sunnah that are not performed in any place except in Medina, and they are prayed in the Mosque of the Messenger of Allah (sw) on the two Eids before going out to the prayer ground. This is unique to Medina because the Messenger of Allah (sw) performed it”.
records.arabicText: 1471 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ اَلْفَضْلِ اَلْهَاشِمِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «رَكْعَتَانِ مِنَ اَلسُّنَّةِ لَيْسَ تُصَلَّيَانِ فِي مَوْضِعٍ إِلاَّ بِالْمَدِينَةِ وَ تُصَلَّى فِي مَسْجِدِ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فِي اَلْعِيدَيْنِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُخْرَجَ إِلَى اَلْمُصَلَّى لَيْسَ ذَلِكَ إِلاَّ بِالْمَدِينَةِ لِأَنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَعَلَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1471 - Muhammad ibn al-Fadl al-Hashimi narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There are two (units of prayer) from the Sunnah that are not performed in any place except in Medina, and they are prayed in the Mosque of the Messenger of Allah (sw) on the two Eids before going out to the prayer ground. This is unique to Medina because the Messenger of Allah (sw) performed it”.

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1473 - Al-Halabi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about the Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha if they fall on a Jumu’ah.
Imam (as) said: "This happened during the time of Imam Ali (as), and He said: 'Whoever wishes to attend the Jumu’ah prayer may do so, and whoever stays back, it will not harm him, but he should pray the midday (Dhuhr) prayer.'
Imam Ali (as) delivered two sermons, combining the Eid sermon and the Jumu’ah sermon”.
records.arabicText: 1473 - وَ سَأَلَ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْفِطْرِ وَ اَلْأَضْحَى إِذَا اِجْتَمَعَا يَوْمَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ قَالَ «اِجْتَمَعَا فِي زَمَانِ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ «مَنْ شَاءَ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ اَلْجُمُعَةَ فَلْيَأْتِ وَ مَنْ قَعَدَ فَلاَ يَضُرُّهُ وَ لْيُصَلِّ اَلظُّهْرَ» وَ خَطَبَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ خُطْبَتَيْنِ جَمَعَ فِيهِمَا خُطْبَةَ اَلْعِيدِ وَ خُطْبَةَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1473 - Al-Halabi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about the Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha if they fall on a Jumu’ah.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said: "This happened during the time of Imam Ali (as), and He said: 'Whoever wishes to attend the Jumu’ah prayer may do so, and whoever stays back, it will not harm him, but he should pray the midday (Dhuhr) prayer.'
Imam Ali (as) delivered two sermons, combining the Eid sermon and the Jumu’ah sermon”.

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1474 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the verse of Allah (swt), the Exalted, "Successful indeed are those who purify themselves," (Surah Al-A'la 87:14).
Imam (as) said: "This refers to the one who gives the Fitrah (charity for breaking the fast)."
He was then asked about the verse, "and remember the Name of their Lord (azj), and pray." (Surah Al-A'la 87:15).
Imam (as) said: "This means going out to the prayer ground and performing the prayer."
records.arabicText: 1474 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنْ قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ: «قَدْ أَفْلَحَ مَنْ تَزَكّٰى» قَالَ «مَنْ أَخْرَجَ اَلْفِطْرَةَ» فَقِيلَ لَهُ «وَ ذَكَرَ اِسْمَ رَبِّهِ فَصَلّٰى» قَالَ «خَرَجَ إِلَى اَلْجَبَّانَةِ فَصَلَّى.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1474 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about the verse of Allah (swt), the Exalted, "Successful indeed are those who purify themselves," (Surah Al-A'la 87:14).
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"This refers to the one who gives the Fitrah (charity for breaking the fast)."
He was then asked about the verse, "and remember the Name of their Lord (azj), and pray." (Surah Al-A'la 87:15).
Imam (as) said: "This means going out to the prayer ground and performing the prayer."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1475 - In the narration of al-Sakuni, it is reported that
"When The Prophet (sw) went out for the Eid prayer, he would not return by the same path he took to go out; he would take a different path."
records.arabicText: 1475 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ: «أَنَّ اَلنَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ كَانَ إِذَا خَرَجَ إِلَى اَلْعِيدِ لَمْ يَرْجِعْ فِي اَلطَّرِيقِ اَلَّذِي بَدَأَ فِيهِ يَأْخُذُ فِي طَرِيقٍ غَيْرِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1475 - In the narration of al-Sakuni, it is reported that
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When The Prophet (sw) went out for the Eid prayer, he would not return by the same path he took to go out; he would take a different path."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1476 - Abu Basir narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"If you intend to travel on the day of Eid and dawn breaks while you are still in the town, then do not leave until you have witnessed that Eid”.
records.arabicText: 1476 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «إِذَا أَرَدْتَ اَلشُّخُوصَ فِي يَوْمِ اَلْعِيدِ فَانْفَجَرَ اَلْفَجْرُ وَ أَنْتَ فِي اَلْبَلَدِ فَلاَ تَخْرُجْ حَتَّى تَشْهَدَ ذَلِكَ اَلْعِيدَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1476 - Abu Basir narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you intend to travel on the day of Eid and dawn breaks while you are still in the town, then do not leave until you have witnessed that Eid”.

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1477 - Sa'd ibn Sa'd narrated from al-Ridha (as) regarding a traveler to Mecca or elsewhere, asking if the traveler is obligated to perform the Eid prayers (Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha).
He (Imam (as)) said: "Yes, except in Mina on the Day of Sacrifice (Eid al-Adha)."
records.arabicText: 1477 - وَ رَوَى سَعْدُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ عَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: فِي اَلْمُسَافِرِ إِلَى مَكَّةَ وَ غَيْرِهَا هَلْ عَلَيْهِ صَلاَةُ اَلْعِيدَيْنِ اَلْفِطْرِ وَ اَلْأَضْحَى قَالَ «نَعَمْ إِلاَّ بِمِنًى يَوْمَ اَلنَّحْرِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1477 - Sa'd ibn Sa'd narrated from al-Ridha (as) regarding a traveler to Mecca or elsewhere, asking if the traveler is obligated to perform the Eid prayers (Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha).
records.thaqalaynMatn: He (Imam (as)) said: "Yes, except in Mina on the Day of Sacrifice (Eid al-Adha)."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1478 - Jabir narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that The Prophet (sw) said:
"When the first day of Shawwal (Eid al-Fitr) arrives, a caller announces, 'O’ believers, proceed to your rewards.'"
Then He (the Prophet) said: "O’ Jabir, the rewards of Allah (swt) are not like the rewards of these kings."
He (the Prophet) said: "It is the Day of Rewards."
records.arabicText: 1478 - وَ رَوَى جَابِرٌ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «إِذَا كَانَ أَوَّلُ يَوْمٍ مِنْ شَوَّالٍ نَادَى مُنَادٍ يَا أَيُّهَا اَلْمُؤْمِنُونَ اُغْدُوا إِلَى جَوَائِزِكُمْ» ثُمَّ قَالَ «يَا جَابِرُ جَوَائِزُ اَللَّهِ لَيْسَتْ كَجَوَائِزِ هَؤُلاَءِ اَلْمُلُوكِ» ثُمَّ قَالَ «هُوَ يَوْمُ اَلْجَوَائِزِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1478 - Jabir narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that The Prophet (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When the first day of Shawwal (Eid al-Fitr) arrives, a caller announces, 'O’ believers, proceed to your rewards.'"
Then He (the Prophet) said: "O’ Jabir, the rewards of Allah (swt) are not like the rewards of these kings."
He (the Prophet) said: "It is the Day of Rewards."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1479 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali (as) observed people playing and laughing on the day of Eid al-Fitr. He turned to his companions and said:
"Verily, Allah (swt), the Exalted, made the month of Ramadan as a racetrack for His creation, in which they compete in obedience to Him to attain His pleasure. In it, some people have raced ahead and won, while others have lagged behind and failed. So, it is truly astonishing to see those who laugh and play on a day when the doers of good are rewarded and the negligent are deprived. By Allah (swt), if the veil were removed, the doer of good would be preoccupied with his good deeds and the wrongdoer would be preoccupied with his wrongdoings."
records.arabicText: 1479 - وَ نَظَرَ اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِلَى أُنَاسٍ فِي يَوْمِ فِطْرٍ يَلْعَبُونَ وَ يَضْحَكُونَ فَقَالَ لِأَصْحَابِهِ وَ اِلْتَفَتَ إِلَيْهِمْ «إِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ جَعَلَ شَهْرَ رَمَضَانَ مِضْمَاراً لِخَلْقِهِ يَسْتَبِقُونَ فِيهِ بِطَاعَتِهِ إِلَى رِضْوَانِهِ فَسَبَقَ فِيهِ قَوْمٌ فَفَازُوا وَ تَخَلَّفَ آخَرُونَ فَخَابُوا فَالْعَجَبُ كُلُّ اَلْعَجَبِ مِنَ اَلضَّاحِكِ اَللاَّعِبِ فِي اَلْيَوْمِ اَلَّذِي يُثَابُ فِيهِ اَلْمُحْسِنُونَ وَ يَخِيبُ فِيهِ اَلْمُقَصِّرُونَ وَ اَيْمُ اَللَّهِ لَوْ كُشِفَ اَلْغِطَاءُ لَشُغِلَ مُحْسِنٌ بِإِحْسَانِهِ وَ مُسِيءٌ بِإِسَاءَتِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1479 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali (as) observed people playing and laughing on the day of Eid al-Fitr. He turned to his companions and said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Verily, Allah (swt), the Exalted, made the month of Ramadan as a racetrack for His creation, in which they compete in obedience to Him to attain His pleasure. In it, some people have raced ahead and won, while others have lagged behind and failed. So, it is truly astonishing to see those who laugh and play on a day when the doers of good are rewarded and the negligent are deprived. By Allah (swt), if the veil were removed, the doer of good would be preoccupied with his good deeds and the wrongdoer would be preoccupied with his wrongdoings."

records.chapter: The Prayer of the Two Eids
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1480 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said: "There is no Eid for the Muslims, whether it is Eid al-Adha or Eid al-Fitr, except that it renews grief for the family of Muhammad."
He was asked: "Why is that?"
Imam (as) said: "Because they see their rightful position in the hands of others”.
records.arabicText: 1480 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَا مِنْ عِيدٍ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ أَضْحًى وَ لاَ فِطْرٍ إِلاَّ وَ هُوَ يُجَدَّدُ فِيهِ لآِلِ مُحَمَّدٍ حُزْنٌ» قِيلَ وَ لِمَ ذَلِكَ قَالَ «لِأَنَّهُمْ يَرَوْنَ حَقَّهُمْ فِي يَدِ غَيْرِهِمْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1480 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"There is no Eid for the Muslims, whether it is Eid al-Adha or Eid al-Fitr, except that it renews grief for the family of Muhammad."
He was asked: "Why is that?"
Imam (as) said: "Because they see their rightful position in the hands of others”.
